column1: What does a conditional allow a program to do?
column2: Choose which code runs based on a true or false condition

column1: What does if do in Python?
column2: It checks a condition and runs its block when the condition is true

column1: What does else do in Python?
column2: It runs when none of the previous conditions were true

column1: What does elif do in Python?
column2: It checks another condition after a previous if condition was false

column1: What is a condition?
column2: An expression that evaluates to True or False

column1: What does answer == yes check?
column2: It checks whether answer is equal to yes

column1: Why does Python not run every branch in an if elif else chain?
column2: Python runs only the first block with a true condition

column1: What happens if the if condition is false and there is an else block?
column2: Python skips the if block and runs the else block

column1: Why is elif useful?
column2: It lets the program check more than one specific condition

column1: What should else usually handle?
column2: Unexpected cases or a final default response

column1: Are Python string comparisons exact?
column2: Yes strings must match exactly

column1: Does Yes match yes in Python?
column2: No because uppercase and lowercase letters are different

column1: Does yes with a space match yes in Python?
column2: No because the space makes it a different string

column1: What defines a code block in Python?
column2: Indentation

column1: Why does indentation matter in Python?
column2: It tells Python which statements belong to the same block

column1: Can a boolean variable be used directly in an if statement?
column2: Yes because it already evaluates to True or False

column1: Why does if is_open not need == True?
column2: Because is_open is already a boolean value

column1: What happens when is_open is False in an if else statement?
column2: Python skips the if block and runs the else block

column1: What was the project goal for Lesson 005?
column2: Use conditionals to respond to the reader choice

column1: What should the Library project do when the user types yes?
column2: Print a message showing that the book selection will be shown

column1: What should the Library project do when the user types no?
column2: Print a polite goodbye message

column1: What should the Library project do for an unexpected response?
column2: Ask the user to type yes or no

column1: Why should variable names match the meaning of the value?
column2: Clear names make the program easier to understand

column1: Why is library_open_response clearer than library_open for user input?
column2: It shows the value is a text response from the user

column1: Why is is_open a good name for a boolean variable?
column2: It sounds like a true or false condition
